specific_ects_requirements.condition_comment: This can be fulfilled also by completing an industrial internship.

english_requirements.notes: Native speaker, English-language Bachelor's degree, or English as a first language at the German 'Abitur' level are also accepted.

course_content_summary: The Master's programme includes experimental and theoretical courses in quantum optics, quantum matter, laser cooling, quantum gases, laser physics, superconductors, Josephson junctions, open and many-body quantum systems, mathematical quantum theory, and quantum information theory. Practical lab work covers lasers, elements of quantum optics, superconductors, photons, and photon statistics. Students also choose modules from a neighbouring field or complete an industrial internship.

program_name: Advisory and Innovation Services in Agri-Food Systems
course_content_summary: This program focuses on establishing and promoting sustainable practices in agri-food systems through professional advice and innovation. It combines practical expertise in agricultural production, economics, and engineering with social science skills for consulting. Key topics include farm management, technical specialization, supporting problem-solving and innovation processes, understanding economic factors in decision-making, and analyzing agricultural knowledge and innovation systems (AKIS). Students learn tools for planning, implementing, and evaluating policies, as well as coordinating innovation networks. The curriculum prepares graduates for careers in consulting, agricultural administration, organizational development in the agri-food sector, and international cooperation.

english_requirements.notes: For more information and other language tests that can be recognised, please refer to the application information website.

course_content_summary: This research-based program focuses on the economic, agricultural, and development policy relationships within the global agricultural and food economy, environment, and rural areas. Key topics include economic growth, sustainability, and social justice in an international context. The curriculum emphasizes a strong foundation in economic and agricultural theories, quantitative and qualitative research methods, policy analysis with sustainability integration, and interdisciplinary analysis of complex systems. Graduates are prepared for careers in governmental and non-governmental organizations, international bodies, and the private sector, working on issues along the agri-food value chain.

program_name: Agricultural Sciences in the Tropics and Subtropics (AgriTropics)
course_content_summary: The AgriTropics program focuses on development-oriented agricultural research for tropical and subtropical regions. Key topics include sustainable and agroecological farming systems, climate change adaptation, resource management, and improving livelihoods for smallholder farmers. The curriculum is interdisciplinary, covering natural resources, material cycles (water, nitrogen, carbon), post-harvest loss reduction, product processing, and socio-economic aspects like gender-sensitive development, extension services, and market access. Students are prepared for careers in international research centers, development cooperation, environmental protection, consulting, and NGOs.

program_name: Air Quality Control, Solid Waste and Waste Water Process Engineering (MSc WASTE)

german_requirements.notes: International students without prior knowledge of German must attend the intensive German language course in September (free of charge).

course_content_summary: This four-semester program focuses on environmental and process engineering. The first semester covers fundamental compulsory modules. In the second and third semesters, students specialize in one of three areas: Air Quality Control, Solid Waste, or Waste Water Process Engineering. Each specialization includes a core module, elective modules, and practical work or an industrial internship. Students can create an individual study plan, incorporating additional electives, a student research project, or an industrial internship. The curriculum is supplemented by excursions and practical work in university research facilities. The fourth semester is dedicated to the Master's thesis, which can be completed at the university, a company, or another research institution. The program also includes mandatory German language courses for non-proficient students and offers a double degree option with the Universidade Federal do Paraná (UFPR) in Brazil.

The program provides comprehensive training in the methods and theories of American Studies, covering the literatures, cultures, history, and society of the United States. Course topics range from America's colonial past to contemporary themes in race, class, gender, religious, ethnic, and regional studies. The curriculum is interdisciplinary, emphasizing student autonomy and academic collaboration. Core components include American Literary and Cultural Studies, research skills, writing, and a thesis colloquium. The program also integrates practical experience through applied American Studies, such as organizing exhibitions and festivals, and encourages internships. Graduates are prepared for careers in academia, journalism, publishing, public relations, consulting, and international organizations.

This program focuses on sustainable and resource-efficient practices in animal science, emphasizing animal welfare. Key topics include the breeding, husbandry, nutrition, and health of farm animals for the production of high-quality foodstuffs. Specializations are available in areas like nutrition and feed, genomics and breeding, and health and behavior. A significant research focus is on the microbiota of the digestive tract and its influence on the animal and environment. The curriculum integrates core pillars of animal science: animal nutrition, hygiene, behavior, breeding, and husbandry, supported by foundational knowledge in anatomy, physiology, endocrinology, genetics, microbiology, and feed science. The course also addresses sustainability, ethical issues, and the social, economic, and environmental impacts of agricultural science, preparing graduates for leadership roles in research, development, and management within biotechnology, agriculture, and the food industry.

specific_ects_requirements.condition_comment: The Bachelor's curriculum must have included courses in these subjects.

course_content_summary: The Applied & Environmental Geoscience (AEG) program is an international, research-oriented, and interdisciplinary curriculum focusing on the evaluation and solution of environmental problems, particularly in the subsurface. Key topics include pollution of drinking water, sustainable use of natural resources, and the impacts of climate and land-use change on soil and water quality. The curriculum is built on a foundation of classical natural sciences (physics, chemistry, biology) combined with modern technology. Core fields of study are hydrogeology, environmental modelling, environmental chemistry, environmental microbiology, and environmental physics. Students can specialize in one of three areas: Hydrogeology, Environmental Chemistry and Environmental Microbiology, or Environmental Physics. The program requires a strong background in mathematics, chemistry, and physics.

specific_ects_requirements.domain_name: Mathematics, Computer Science, and Artificial Intelligence
specific_ects_requirements.condition_comment: A minimum of 10 ECTS credits is required in each of the three subject areas.

accepted_tests.test_name: ICC (Certificate in English for Business Purposes)

english_requirements.notes: Native speakers are exempt. Applicants with a degree from an English-language study programme are exempt. The certificate must not be older than three years.

course_content_summary: The Master's programme in Applied Artificial Intelligence (MAAI) provides in-depth training in AI technologies with two main tracks: Application and Research. Key topics include machine learning, data analysis, and artificial neural networks. The curriculum focuses on the practical application of AI in various sectors such as healthcare, engineering, logistics, business informatics, and software engineering, aiming to educate highly qualified professionals capable of applying and advancing these technologies across industries.

english_requirements.notes: If applicants have studied completely in English in their previous studies, the test may be waived. Applicants may also be interviewed to test their English skills.

course_content_summary: The curriculum includes modules such as International Project Management, Software Architecture, IT Security, Advanced Database, Software Development Practice, and Advanced Computer Science. The program focuses on full stack development, covering front end, middleware, back end, databases, and infrastructure. Students can choose individual specializations in Business Computing, Mobile Computing, and Usability Engineering and UX Design. The degree prepares graduates for jobs in the software and IT industry, public sector, research, and for doctoral studies.

program_name: Applied Data Science and Artificial Intelligence

english_requirements.notes: If applicants have studied completely in English in their previous studies, we may waive the required test.

course_content_summary: The programme focuses on qualifying students as business application data scientists. Key topics include big data architectures, data and text mining algorithms, neural networks, and predictive analytics. It also covers creative questioning techniques, data visualisation, data storytelling, ethics, and international law. The curriculum integrates practical experience through a series of three case studies with increasing complexity, culminating in a Master's thesis, ideally on an industry-related topic.

program_name: Architecture – Design for the Built Environment (MA)

specific_ects_requirements.condition_comment: Applicants with a 180 ECTS degree can acquire an additional 30 ECTS through a preliminary course.

english_requirements.notes: If applicants have studied completely in English in their previous studies, we may waive the required test.

course_content_summary: The program focuses on a reflexive approach to architecture and the city. Students develop architectural ideas, concepts, and theories, and implement them in design projects within specific urban contexts. The teaching methodology is project-based, enhanced by field trips, research projects, and academic events like summer schools and conferences.

english_requirements.notes: Native speaker, English-language Bachelor's degree, or English as the first language at the German "Abitur" level are also accepted.

course_content_summary: This is a research-oriented two-year Master's programme focusing on Astro and Particle Physics, managed by the Kepler Center. The curriculum connects science from particle physics, astrophysics, and cosmology, combining disciplines in experimental and theoretical physics, astronomy, and astrophysics. Students will explore the origin, structure, and evolution of the universe and the properties of elementary particles under extreme conditions. The program is closely linked to a PhD programme on 'Particles, Fields and Messengers of the Universe'.

english_requirements.notes: Evidence of very good written and spoken English is required.

course_content_summary: This program focuses on the fundamentals of life and disease, combining biology and chemistry. Key topics include the study of nucleic acids, proteins, lipids, and sugars in forming cells and organisms. The curriculum is flexible and covers a broad range of life sciences, including immunology, cell biology, molecular and structural biology, biophysics, virology, microbiology, pathobiochemistry, toxicology, pharmacology, bioinformatics, developmental biology, neuroscience, and molecular medicine. A strong emphasis is placed on practical laboratory proficiency, organisational skills, scientific writing, and presentation, preparing students for an international career in research.

This interdisciplinary program focuses on the entire value chain of bio-based products. Key topics include the production of biological raw materials in diverse agricultural ecosystems, supply logistics, and the biotechnological and industrial processes for converting these resources into new products. Students will analyze the environmental, social, and economic dimensions of the bioeconomy from both micro and macro perspectives, considering innovation, institutions, and policies. The curriculum integrates knowledge from Agricultural Sciences, Natural Sciences, and Business, Economics, and Social Sciences, equipping graduates to understand bio-based value chains from the perspectives of companies, producers, market actors, and supporting organizations.

german_requirements.notes: Required for Variant B and C students.

course_content_summary: The Master's degree programme in bioinformatics integrates courses in bioinformatics, computer science, and life sciences. The curriculum is designed to equip graduates with the skills to solve biological problems using high throughput technologies and bioinformatics methods. Core competencies include algorithmic thinking, efficient analysis, data visualisation, and mass data storage. The program has a strong research and hands-on focus. Specialized subjects include genomics, transcriptomics, protein and drug design, microbiome analysis, and systems biology.

english_requirements.notes: Applicants with a Bachelor's degree from countries whose native language is English do not have to prove their English language skills, e.g., USA, Canada, UK, etc.

course_content_summary: The program offers a general Biological Sciences track and three priority tracks: Cellular & Molecular Biology, Disease Biology, and Ecology, Evolutionary & Behavioural Biology. Key topics in Cellular & Molecular Biology include biochemistry, genetics, biophysics, and macromolecules. The Disease Biology track covers immunology, infections, cell death, toxicology, stem cell research, cancer research, and neurodegenerative diseases. The Ecology, Evolution & Behaviour track focuses on animal behaviour, ecology, evolution, physiology, and neurobiology. The curriculum emphasizes research methods, is taught in English in small groups, and includes a mandatory internship of at least two months. A fast-track option to a PhD is available for nominated students.

specific_ects_requirements.condition_comment: At least 75 points required from the listed fields.

english_requirements.notes: Native speakers from specific countries are exempt. Exemption also possible for those who completed a Bachelor's or Master's programme with English as the language of instruction in an EU country or other specified countries.

course_content_summary: This interdisciplinary, research-oriented program focuses on the properties, production processes, and applications of enzymes and microorganisms, particularly in the food industry and for bioanalytical purposes. Core topics include enzymatic production, biochemical methods such as enzyme purification, characterization, and immobilization, enzyme kinetics, gene expression, and mutagenesis of recombinant enzymes. The curriculum also covers pathogens, hygiene in food production, and the safe application of biotechnology in the life science industry.

The curriculum includes functional neuroanatomy, sensory and motor systems, neurophysiology, cellular and molecular biology of neurons and glia, neurochemistry, neurotransmitters, neurogenetics, and developmental neurobiology. It also covers the genetic and molecular basis of neurological and psychiatric diseases, neuroglia, microscopy, molecular imaging techniques, tissue culture, tissue engineering, and stem cells. The program features two laboratory rotations and a Master's thesis.

english_requirements.notes: For non-native speakers. Graduates of English-language Bachelor's study programmes (minimum six semesters) are exempt with a certificate from their university.

course_content_summary: A research-oriented Master's programme focusing on advanced, hands-on laboratory training and independent research. Students can specialize in four main research profiles: Sustainable Synthesis and Catalysis, Smart Materials and Functional Molecules, Biological Chemistry and Biotechnology, and Theory and Simulation in Chemistry and Materials Sciences. The curriculum is designed to deepen conceptual understanding and analytical skills in chemistry.

english_requirements.notes: Applicants with a Bachelor's degree from Australia, the United Kingdom, Ireland, Canada, Malta, New Zealand, Singapore, South Africa or the United States do not have to prove their English language skills.

course_content_summary: The Master's programme focuses on advanced, research-related courses in inorganic, organic, and physical chemistry. It also covers the department's main research areas of life sciences and material sciences as compulsory elective subjects. A key feature is the 'focus area courses,' which involve practical experience and research projects under intensive supervision, providing a strong foundation for the Master's thesis and subsequent doctoral studies.

required_degree_domains: Biology with a specialisation in Neurosciences

english_requirements.notes: A higher education degree in a study programme with English as the sole language of instruction is accepted. A CEFR C1 level or higher shown on the higher education entrance qualification is also accepted.

course_content_summary: This interdisciplinary program integrates Engineering, Computer Science, and Psychology to explore cognitive systems in both humans and machines. Key topics include perception, learning, memory, reasoning, decision-making, and intelligent interaction. Students will engage in theoretical, empirical, and computational approaches, including psychophysical studies, psychological testing, simulations, and modeling. The curriculum focuses on real-world applications such as intelligent robotics, cognitive assistance systems, smart vehicles, AI in healthcare, and human-computer interaction, preparing graduates for careers in these cutting-edge fields or for further academic research.

specific_ects_requirements.condition_comment: Basic knowledge in six or more of the following eight topics, to the extent of at least one module each.

english_requirements.notes: A higher education degree in a study programme with English as the sole language of instruction is accepted. A CEFR C1 level or higher shown on the higher education entrance qualification is also accepted.

course_content_summary: The Communication and Information Technology program offers four specializations: Communications Circuits and Systems, Sensor Circuits and Systems, Communications Engineering, and Intelligent Systems. Core topics include integrated mixed-signal and RF circuits, RF engineering, radar design, wireless and mobile communication, signal processing, optical communication devices, and machine learning. The Communications Engineering track focuses on digital methods like coding, networking, and advanced modulation. The Communications Circuits and Systems track emphasizes hardware aspects such as microwave/millimetre-wave engineering, microelectronics, and optoelectronic devices. The Sensor Circuits and Systems specialization covers integrated circuit and sensor design, including mm-wave engineering and sensor interfaces. The Intelligent Systems track is software-oriented, focusing on machine learning, pattern recognition, and secure system engineering.

program_name: Comparative and Middle East Politics and Society (CMEPS)

english_requirements.notes: Exemptions for native speakers or those with previous study experience in English-language programmes.

german_requirements.notes: German language skills are not a prerequisite.

course_content_summary: The CMEPS program offers two profiles: a 'Political Science' profile for students with an area studies background, focusing on research methods, political science theories, and advanced Arabic; and an 'Arabic' profile for political science students, featuring intensive beginner's Arabic. Core courses for all students include Comparative Politics of the Middle East, Regime Change and Democratisation, Comparative Theories of Political Science, and International Cooperation and Development. Electives cover topics such as international relations, political economy of the Middle East, international migration governance, social movements, development cooperation, conflict analysis, and the political impact of new media. The program includes a mandatory semester abroad at the partner university (American University in Cairo), an internship, and culminates in a Master's thesis with a joint colloquium.

english_requirements.notes: MoI is not accepted.

german_requirements.notes: The programme is taught in English.

course_content_summary: The program covers dialogue systems, machine translation, and search engines, focusing on the automatic processing of written and spoken language. It offers three specialization areas: core computational linguistics, applied natural language processing, and speech processing. Students gain practical skills through a team laboratory project and prepare for their thesis in a research module. Electives are available from the Institute for Natural Language Processing, Computer Science, and Linguistics departments.

english_requirements.notes: Applicants with a German Abitur (final grade 'good' in English), native English speakers, or those who graduated from a school where English was the language of instruction in an English-speaking country are exempt.

course_content_summary: The program focuses on current themes in computational linguistics research and practical skills. Key topics include grammar formalisms, machine learning, information retrieval, dialectometry, and computer-aided language learning. The curriculum emphasizes project-driven work and academic problem-solving.

program_name: Computational Mechanics of Materials and Structures (COMMAS)
course_content_summary: The Computational Mechanics of Materials and Structures (COMMAS) programme focuses on developing and implementing numerical methods and simulation techniques for complex engineering problems. Students learn to model, understand, predict, and validate the responses of materials and structures. This interdisciplinary program integrates Civil Engineering, Mechanical Engineering, and Aerospace Engineering. Practical applications include simulating automobile crash tests, predicting material failure loads, modeling biological tissues, and developing functional materials for robotics. As a research-oriented programme, it prepares graduates for careers at universities, laboratories, and research centers.

The curriculum covers neural data analysis, models of neural coding and computation, physical and physiological bases of neural recordings, brain imaging, computational motor control, and computational vision. Specialized topics include rehabilitation robotics, brain-computer interfaces, systems neuroscience, and neurophysiology. Foundational courses in mathematics, statistics, programming, and machine learning for neuroscience are provided. Students also explore theoretical neuroscience, behaviour, cognition, and basic mechanisms of learning and memory. The program includes practical components like an essay rotation, a laboratory rotation, and a Master's thesis in student-selected labs.

english_requirements.notes: The language of instruction is English. A level of at least high B2 is expected.

course_content_summary: The Master's program in Computer and Information Science focuses on specialization in areas related to the exploration of large data volumes. Key topics include Data Science, Visual Computing, and Human-Computer Interaction (HCI). The curriculum has a strong research orientation, emphasizing exploratory analysis and visualization of large information spaces. Students can specialize in Data Science, Interactive Systems, Software and Systems, Visual Computing, and Algorithmics. The program includes a mentoring component where students are guided by a professor in their chosen field of specialization.

english_requirements.notes: MoI is not accepted.

german_requirements.notes: Proof for German language proficiency is not required as the programme is taught in English.

course_content_summary: The Master's in Computer Science at the University of Stuttgart offers specializations in three majors: Autonomous Systems (AUT), Service Technology and Engineering (STE), and Visual Computing (VC). The curriculum is research-oriented, preparing students for PhD programs and industry roles. Core topics include robotics, computer engineering, database systems, parallel and distributed systems. While obligatory courses are in English, students can choose elective courses in German. The program benefits from strong ties to the local technology industry, including companies like Daimler, Porsche, and Bosch.

program_name: Computer Science for Industry and Business (MSc COSIB)

english_requirements.notes: Waivers include: 5 years of continuous English classes in German secondary school (Abitur level); completion of 4 subject-related courses in English (or 3 courses + thesis in English) in a previous degree from the EEA or Switzerland; a degree or exchange semester from an English-speaking country (USA, UK, Canada, Ireland, Australia, New Zealand, South Africa).

course_content_summary: This one-year continuing education program offers a compact, career-oriented qualification in computer science for STEM graduates with professional experience. Core content includes the principles of algorithms, data structures, and programming languages. A key specialization is the Data Science profile, which covers analysis, processing, and visualization of data, particularly in the context of big data and machine learning. The curriculum emphasizes practical application through an integrated eight-week compulsory internship and the opportunity to write the Master's thesis in a company, preparing students for the German IT labor market.

english_requirements.notes: Proof of having completed the first degree programme entirely in English is also accepted.

course_content_summary: This program provides a comprehensive focus on corporate communication management, covering three main pillars: public relations & CSR communication, internal communication & change management, and digital marketing & social media. Students gain in-depth knowledge and applied skills in communication methods, techniques, and leadership. The curriculum is research-based, incorporating current trends and a teaching research project. It also integrates core and elective subjects from contemporary management, strategic management, and leadership, offered by the Business School Pforzheim. The program emphasizes practical application through case studies, company projects, and empirical studies, preparing graduates for managerial and expert roles in corporate communications, PR, and CSR across various sectors.

english_requirements.notes: If applicants have studied completely in English in their previous studies, we may waive the required test. We may also personally interview applicants in order to test their English skills.

course_content_summary: Dance Movement Therapy is an evidence-based arts therapy approach utilizing dance and movement to foster physical, emotional, cognitive, social, and spiritual integration. The curriculum focuses on creative methods to enhance client expression, social connection, and emotional well-being. Professional applications are explored across various fields, including psychiatry, psychosomatic medicine, special education, neurology, oncology, cardiovascular medicine, geriatrics, forensic contexts, and paediatrics. The program also covers its use in preventive care and educational settings like schools and psychosocial institutions for children and families.

program_name: Development, Environment, Societies, and History of South Asia

english_requirements.notes: If your first language is English and you are a citizen of a majority English-speaking country OR you earned your Bachelor's degree in a majority English-speaking country, then you do not need to demonstrate English proficiency.

german_requirements.notes: Evidence of German language proficiency is not needed. Successful completion of the study programme doesn't necessitate German language skills.

course_content_summary: This is a two-year, research-oriented, interdisciplinary Master's program focusing on South Asia (Afghanistan, Bangladesh, Bhutan, India, the Maldives, Nepal, Pakistan, Sri Lanka). Taught in English, the curriculum covers historical, social, political, and economic developments, as well as geography and environmental topics within the region. Students choose a study focus from Political Science, History, Anthropology, Art History and Visual Culture, Development Economics, or Geography. The program emphasizes independent work and self-reflexivity, drawing from paradigms in the humanities and social sciences. It also offers optional language instruction in ten South Asian languages, including Bengali, Dari, Hindi, Sanskrit, and Urdu.

modules.condition_comment: If you do not have the 5 ECTS in Statistics, we offer an additional course in advance to achieve them.

english_requirements.notes: Proof of having completed an academic degree programme entirely in English is accepted.

course_content_summary: This program provides a comprehensive focus on digital project management and the digitalization of companies. Core topics include digital transformation projects, digital management, digital business models and products, project and process management, and algorithms and data analytics. Students will gain in-depth knowledge of process analytics, process design, data analytics, and responsible management methods. The curriculum emphasizes applied skills in project management techniques, tools, and leadership. Elective courses in the third semester allow for individual profile shaping in areas like strategic management.

english_requirements.notes: Exemptions for native speakers from specific countries, applicants with a Bachelor's degree taught in English, those who attended a bilingual school with English as a language of instruction, or those with a certain grade in English from a German Gymnasium.

course_content_summary: This interdisciplinary, research-oriented program links natural sciences with agricultural and economic sciences to provide a comprehensive understanding of Earth system processes. Key topics include climate change, land use, food production, and food security. Students learn to analyze the Earth system's current state and simulate its components, such as regional climate, with a focus on land management and agricultural activities. The curriculum is integrated with international research in weather, climate, and Earth system science. Graduates will be experts in developing sustainability concepts, protecting the environment, and providing guidance on Earth's development to decision-makers and the public.

The program provides a firm background in modern scientific methods of economics and their application to economic problems. Specialization areas include Econometrics and Applied Economics, International Financial Economics, Macroeconomics and International Economics, Microeconomics and Decision-Making, and Public Economics.

program_name: Economics (MSc) with Double Degree Option with the University of Nottingham

english_requirements.notes: German "Abitur" (with at least six/seven years of English), a Bachelor's degree in English/American Studies, or a university degree from the UK, Ireland, USA, Canada, Australia, or New Zealand are accepted. Note: The University of Nottingham requires an IELTS of 6.5 (no less than 6.0 in any element) or equivalent for the double degree option.

german_requirements.notes: German is not required. However, German classes are available.

course_content_summary: The MSc Economics program provides a state-of-the-art education in economics, focusing on preparing students for careers in academia, public, and private sectors. Core knowledge is expected in Microeconomics, Macroeconomics, and Quantitative Methods (Statistics, Mathematics, Econometrics). The curriculum is based on cutting-edge research and addresses contemporary policy issues. Key research and teaching topics include the Effect of ICT Adoption on Firm Organisation, Heterogenous Agent Macroeconomics, International Taxation, Economics of Taxation, Fiscal Policy during the Covid Pandemic, Computational Macroeconomics, and Empirical Topics in International Trade. The program benefits from the university's status as a German university of excellence with a world-leading machine learning cluster.

english_requirements.notes: German "Abitur" with at least six (G8) or seven (G9) years of English, a Bachelor's degree in English/American Studies, or a university degree from the UK, Ireland, USA, Canada, Australia, or New Zealand are also accepted.

german_requirements.notes: Knowledge of German is not required to complete the programme.

course_content_summary: This program combines economics, finance, and econometrics to provide a deep understanding of economic theory, financial instruments for risk management, and modern econometric analysis. Students learn to analyze financial and economic data using statistical software like Matlab, GAUSS, SAS, and Stata. Core topics include the pricing of risky assets, the role of options and futures in social welfare, and managing financial stability. The curriculum develops methodological skills in theoretical modeling, econometrics, and programming, preparing graduates for careers in financial institutions, regulatory bodies, central banks, or academia.

german_requirements.notes: No German language proficiency required.

course_content_summary: This two-year Master's programme, taught entirely in English, covers the traditional foundations of electrical engineering and information technology. Students choose one of seven specialisations: Smart Information Processing, Communication Systems, Nano- and Optoelectronics, Power-Electronic Systems and Technologies, Smart Systems, Electromagnetics and Applications, or Electrical Energy Systems. The curriculum benefits from the university's strong research funding and close connections with industry, facilitating a transition to professional careers.

english_requirements.notes: Medium of Instruction Certificates are not accepted.

course_content_summary: The program covers Applied Mathematics, Numerical Methods, Advanced Control Systems, Communication (Near Field & Wireless), Signal Processing for sensors and actuators, Embedded Computing, and Embedded Control. Specialization profiles include System-On-Chip Design (Circuits & Systems, Computer Architecture) and Sensor Data Processing (Computer Vision, Lidar & Radar Systems, Digital Filters). Electives include Machine Learning and a Research Project, culminating in a Master's Thesis.

The program focuses on methodologically-oriented courses in the core areas of engineering cybernetics, including Control, Robotics, System dynamics, and Modelling and simulation. It emphasizes foundational knowledge and the in-depth use of applied mathematical tools. Students learn to abstract complex problems from various engineering fields like aerospace, mechanical, biomedical, and energy systems. The curriculum covers control analysis and design using methods from systems theory, dynamical systems modelling, simulation, and computational methods.

program_name: Environmental Protection and Agricultural Food Production (EnviroFood)

english_requirements.notes: For more information and other language tests that can be recognised, please refer to the application information website.

course_content_summary: This program focuses on sustainable environmental protection and intensifying food production through environmentally friendly systems. Key topics include the analysis of economic, political, and administrative interactions within agroecosystems, understanding the properties and impact of environmental chemicals, and developing models for environmental systems. Students will learn to conduct simulations, analyze geodata using geographic information systems (GIS), and develop integrative, global problem-solving approaches. The curriculum prepares graduates for careers in environmental and quality management, agricultural consulting, and research and development, emphasizing interdisciplinary skills and teamwork in intercultural settings.

program_name: Environmental Science – Soil, Water and Biodiversity (EnvEuro)

specific_ects_requirements.condition_comment: Proven knowledge required in at least two of the following three areas.

english_requirements.notes: For more information and other language tests that can be recognised, please refer to the application information website.

course_content_summary: This European double Master's programme focuses on the sustainable use and management of natural resources in Europe. It offers specializations in Water Resources, Soil Resources and Land Use, Ecosystems and Biodiversity, Environmental Management, and Climate Change. The program involves studying at two of four partner universities in the Euroleague for Life Sciences, resulting in a full double degree. Key objectives include teaching principles of environmental management of ecosystems, identifying and solving problems related to natural resources in an international context, and fostering international collaboration. Graduates are prepared for careers in environmental and quality management, environmental technology, consultancy, impact assessment, risk analysis, and research in both public and private sectors.

specific_ects_requirements.condition_comment: Must have taken at least four courses in business, including two from the quantitative area.

course_content_summary: This program focuses on global management and digital competencies in an era of digital disruption. Taught entirely in English across two countries (Germany and France, Ireland, Italy, or Canada), it emphasizes intercultural and interdisciplinary teamwork in small class sizes. The curriculum has a strong practical orientation, with input from corporate professionals and professors with extensive industry experience. Core topics include global business strategy, digital transformation, and management skills for international careers. The final semester is dedicated to a master's thesis, which can be combined with a global internship. The program is AACSB accredited.

english_requirements.notes: Applicants are exempt if their Bachelor’s degree was conducted in English.

german_requirements.notes: Intensive German language courses are part of the curriculum.

course_content_summary: The MBA in International Management is a full-time program focusing on key management subjects, intensive German language courses, academic writing, and business research methods. In the second semester, students choose four of ten elective subjects on contemporary management issues. A mandatory three-month in-company project and a three-month thesis provide practical experience, applying knowledge to real-world business challenges. Career paths include marketing and product management, business and strategy development, key account management, controlling, consultancy, international sales, global procurement, logistics, and supply chain management.

english_requirements.notes: German "Abitur" including at least six (G8) or seven (G9) years of English is accepted. An officially recognised higher education degree with English as the language of instruction is also accepted. The University of Nottingham requires an IELTS of 6.5 (no less than 6.0 in any element) or an equivalent qualification.

german_requirements.notes: German is not required. However, German classes are available.

course_content_summary: The MSc in European Economics is a double degree program focusing on graduate-level economics education with an integrated year of study abroad at partner universities in Pavia (Italy), Nottingham (England), or Marseille (France). The curriculum expects foundational knowledge in Microeconomics, Trade Theory and Policy, Finance, and Quantitative Methods (Mathematics, Statistics, Econometrics). The program is designed with an international focus, preparing graduates for careers in international companies or public sector institutions by providing experience with different working procedures and cultures. Students earn a Master's degree from both the University of Tübingen and their chosen partner university.

program_name: Executive Master of International Business Management MBA
course_content_summary: The Executive MBA program focuses on general management, providing a comprehensive knowledge base in all essential functional business areas. It is designed for professionals aiming for middle and top management positions. The curriculum integrates business studies with the development of soft skills, social competencies, and intercultural skills. Key features include a strong practical relevance, an international orientation with modules abroad, and a professional learning environment in small groups. Participants benefit from expert lectures and networking with peers.

specific_ects_requirements.condition_comment: A point-based system is used, at least 70 points required.

english_requirements.notes: Exemptions apply for native speakers from specific countries (UK, Ireland, Australia, Canada, New Zealand, USA, etc.) or for applicants who have completed a Bachelor's or Master's programme with English as the language of instruction in an EU member state or one of the aforementioned countries.

course_content_summary: This research-oriented program focuses on the transformation of plant and animal-based raw materials into value-added, safe, and stable food or nutrient formulas. It covers the interface between complex food matrices and technical processes in food development and production. Key areas include food processing equipment, biogenesis of raw materials, microbiological ecology, development of food formulas and processing techniques, automated production processes, and in-line sensor technology. The curriculum aims to transfer basic research concepts into new technological approaches that meet consumer expectations and individual nutritional requirements.

english_requirements.notes: Proof must not be older than two years.

course_content_summary: This program focuses on the booming healthcare industry, integrating medical, scientific, technical, and economic expertise. It prepares graduates for research, development, and leadership roles at the interface of health and management. Key topics include health economics, business management, and technology management. The curriculum is interdisciplinary, featuring challenging projects, case studies, and a Master's thesis to develop scientific working skills for a wide range of career opportunities in the health tech sector.

english_requirements.notes: Documented B2 level required.

german_requirements.notes: No requirements

course_content_summary: The program provides comprehensive knowledge in AI-orientated Python programming, machine learning, deep learning, and generative AI. It also covers big data, data science, and data engineering. Practical skills are developed using application software from manufacturers like Microsoft, SAP, Siemens, and AWS, with a focus on ERP systems, cloud computing, robotic process automation (RPA), business intelligence, and analytics. Specialisations are offered in 'Industrial Operation and Transformation' (focusing on AI application areas and tools) and 'Artificial Intelligence and Data Engineering' (focusing on the development and refinement of AI tools).

The Information Engineering program focuses on designing and implementing linked, end-to-end IT systems for digital transformation. It covers the entire chain from sensor technology to IT systems and business models. Students learn to design cyber-physical business systems to solve complex socio-technical problems by integrating expertise from sensor technology, information systems, and business management. The curriculum prepares students for roles as innovation-driven, research-qualified information engineers, building upon skills from Informatics or Industrial Engineering bachelor's degrees.

english_requirements.notes: If applicants have studied completely in English in their previous studies, we may waive the required test.

course_content_summary: The program focuses on modern applications of information technology, with key subjects in wireless communications, cellular TV phone technology, satellite technology (including CubeSat standard projects), robotics with a focus on navigation, and network technology. The curriculum is practice-oriented, emphasizing the implementation, support, and management of computer-based information systems, covering both software applications and computer hardware. Students learn to convert, store, protect, process, transmit, and securely retrieve information through hands-on projects in small working groups.

The INFOTECH Master's programme integrates computer science and electronics/information engineering. Key course components include information, communication, embedded systems, and computer engineering. The curriculum is structured with core and supplementary modules, a research project, a lab course, and non-technical modules in the first three semesters. The final semester is dedicated to a six-month Master's thesis, providing students with fundamental methods and scientific skills for research and development in information technology.

The MSc in Integrated Urbanism and Sustainable Design (IUSD) prepares urban practitioners to address environmental, cultural, socio-economic, and governance challenges from global urban transformation. The curriculum is open to graduates from architecture, urban planning, landscape architecture, regional planning, and civil engineering. It emphasizes transnational learning and interdisciplinary teamwork on site-specific projects in collaboration with local communities and international stakeholders. Students develop skills in integrated and transdisciplinary solutions for planning, managing, and designing landscapes, cities, and buildings. The program is a double degree offered jointly with Ain Shams University, Cairo.

required_degree_domains: Similar engineering or natural science degrees

english_requirements.notes: Native speakers and students who passed the entirety of their undergraduate studies in Australia, Canada, Ireland, New Zealand, the US or the UK are exempt from this rule.

course_content_summary: The ITECH programme is a multidisciplinary, research-oriented, and experiment-based Master's focusing on the contemporary built environment. It merges design, engineering, construction, and natural sciences to advance technological and computational processes. Key topics include computational design simulation, advanced fabrication processes, robotics, digital manufacturing, material science, and biology. The curriculum emphasizes a critical and analytical approach to design techniques, methods, and theories, exploring their relationship with engineering, robotics, and material science. Students engage with cutting-edge computational architectural design, structural and climate engineering, and advanced construction technologies. The program aims to prepare students for the complex challenges in the building industry by investigating integrative technological advancements and fostering individual interests in architectural design, structures, technology, and computation.

english_requirements.notes: Native English speakers are exempt (from Australia, Canada, Ireland, New Zealand, UK, or USA). Also exempt with a continuous stay of at least one year in an English-speaking country (proven by studies, employment, etc.).

german_requirements.notes: Not necessary

course_content_summary: The program specializes in the development and application of mechatronic systems using intelligent methods like AI. It covers the interface of mechanics, electronics, and computer science. Key topics include artificial intelligence, machine learning, and intelligent control systems. The curriculum, featuring a wide range of elective courses, aims to develop autonomous, adaptive, and efficient systems for industries such as automotive and robotics. Graduates are prepared for roles in research, development, and industry, focusing on the next generation of intelligent systems.

english_requirements.notes: Students must ensure that they are able to follow a lecture on Master's level in English.

course_content_summary: This application-oriented Master's program focuses on the analysis, structuring, and strategic use of large datasets. Core topics include artificial intelligence and data science, with an emphasis on systems that learn rather than being explicitly programmed. Students will learn to design, understand, and implement complex, distributed, and large-scale intelligent systems for both industry and research applications. The curriculum covers scientific methodologies in application-oriented computer science, preparing graduates for challenging scientific and managerial roles.

english_requirements.notes: An English-language school-leaving certificate, equivalent university entrance qualification, or a degree program of at least three years with English coursework and exams (at least 180 ECTS) are also accepted.

course_content_summary: The program covers internal and external business environments, focusing on human resource management, leadership, global marketing, and international finance and economics. A significant emphasis is placed on intercultural communication. Advanced studies in intercultural management adopt a cross-disciplinary approach. The final stage involves an in-depth empirical research thesis, ideally on real-world business situations with a focus on sustainability or corporate responsibility. The curriculum includes training in quantitative and qualitative research methods. Students have the option to integrate an internship or a study abroad semester.

english_requirements.notes: Proof can also be provided by an English-language degree programme or several years of professional experience in an English-speaking country.

course_content_summary: This MBA program is designed for experienced professionals focusing on international business. Key curriculum areas include finance and accounting, marketing, human resource management, economics, and various facets of management. The course trains students to become experts in global business through a curriculum taught by lecturers with extensive international experience.

english_requirements.notes: Proof that the degree they apply with (Bachelor's/Master's) was conducted completely in English is accepted.

german_requirements.notes: Applicants do not need to provide proof of their German language skills.

course_content_summary: The curriculum covers core financial topics across several semesters. The first semester focuses on Financial Management, including Management Accounting, Financial Modelling, and Applied IFRS, alongside Financial Analysis covering Portfolio Management, Bond, and Equity Analysis. It also includes Management Skills like Strategic and Intercultural Management, and Quantitative Research methods such as Quantitative Risk Management and empirical research design. The second semester delves into Applied International Corporate Finance, with modules on Mergers and Acquisitions, Private Equity, and IPOs, as well as Applied Quantitative Corporate Finance focusing on Company Valuation and imperfect capital markets. Advanced topics include Derivatives, Financial Engineering, and Empirical Finance with Predictive Analytics. The third semester offers specialization in Innovation in the Financial Industry, covering Digital Finance, FinTech, Data Science, and Prompt Engineering for finance and investment analysis, culminating in a Master's thesis. An optional double degree program is also available.

english_requirements.notes: Waivers: First degree taught in English or with a major in English, GMAT score of at least 500, native speakers, or citizens from countries where English is an official language. An internal university English test is also possible.

german_requirements.notes: A completed degree programme taught in German is accepted as proof.

course_content_summary: The MBA in International Management covers a comprehensive range of business disciplines. Core modules include Accounting (Financial, Management), Quantitative Finance (Methods, Investment), and General Management with a focus on corporate strategic planning. Students will study Project and Organisation Management, Value Chain Management (Marketing, Supply Chain, Purchasing), and Managerial Economics (Micro and Macro). The curriculum also features International Business Law, International Management, and Human Resource Management. Advanced topics include International Financial Resources (Reporting, Control, Finance), International Value Chain Management with a focus on marketing and intercultural competence, and Sustainable International Trade. The program culminates in strategy case studies and a Master's thesis. Elective options allow for specialization in areas such as International Supply Chain Management, Business Analytics and AI, Sustainability Management, Innovation, and Entrepreneurship.

university_name: HTWG Konstanz - University of Applied Sciences
required_degree_domains: interdisciplinary degree related to Asia and/or business
course_content_summary: The International Management Asia-Europe program is a three-semester Master of Arts focusing on executive management skills at the intersection of Europe and Asia. Core topics include complexity and risk management in international business. The program features a favorable student-instructor ratio and small group sizes, building on a 25-year legacy in Asian Studies. A double-degree option extends the program to four semesters.

english_requirements.notes: The university plans to update the English entry requirement to C1 level soon.

german_requirements.notes: No German language requirement at the time of entry. However, an A2 certificate must be submitted by the end of the second semester. German courses are part of the programme.

course_content_summary: The IMLA programme focuses on practice-oriented landscape architecture with five main subject areas: planning and designing projects at various scales, international planning and design, planning and project management, information technology in planning and design, and planning and design methods. It emphasizes a strong practical focus, dealing with European countries and cross-border issues. Key topics include landscape as a system and projects conducted in various European locations.

specific_ects_requirements.condition_comment: combined

english_requirements.notes: German Abitur with minimum five years of English until the final year with an average grade of five points within the last two years or in the final exam. A Bachelor's degree taught in English, a university entrance qualification from an English programme, or a Bachelor's thesis in English may also be accepted.

course_content_summary: This research-oriented Master's program in Geophysics focuses on exploration and earthquake seismology, and natural hazards. The curriculum combines theory with practical field experiments. Core compulsory courses cover earthquake seismology and seismics, which can be supplemented by optional courses, field classes, summer schools, and workshops. Students gain expertise across the full range of geophysics, including data acquisition, modelling, inversion, and interpretation. The program is taught entirely in English.

program_name: International Master's Programme in Infrastructure Planning – MIP

english_requirements.notes: Confirmations from universities (English as the medium of instruction) are not accepted. The test must not be older than 24 months at the time of application.

course_content_summary: This interdisciplinary program focuses on infrastructure planning for development, covering transport, water, and waste management at urban and regional levels. Core modules include Integrated Planning, Transportation Planning and Modelling, Regional and Urban Planning, Economics, Statistics, GIS, Data Acquisition, Project Management (Tendering, Contracting), Development Policy, Ecological Aspects, Energy Supply, Water and Waste Management, and Project Appraisal and Financing. The curriculum integrates economic, social, ecological, and management perspectives, emphasizing international cooperation and practical application through case studies.

university_name: Hochschule für Technik Stuttgart - University of Applied Sciences

english_requirements.notes: Native English speakers are exempt.

course_content_summary: This program is for professionals in construction, infrastructure, or engineering, preparing them for leadership in complex international projects. It offers two specializations: International Building Project Management (IBPM), focusing on structuring and managing superstructure projects, and International Infrastructure Technology & Management (IITM), which equips students with skills for large-scale infrastructure projects. The curriculum covers general project management, specialized topics, soft skills, and the latest tools, processes, and organizational methods. An optional additional semester in the UK allows students to earn an MBA from Edinburgh Napier University.

This program focuses on landscape ecology to address global environmental change, examining the dynamics of biodiversity and ecosystems. Core topics include the relationships between biodiversity, climate, soils, land use, and nature conservation. The curriculum integrates hands-on experience through fieldwork, laboratory experiments, and computer-based activities, with a strong emphasis on project and team-based work. Students develop quantitative skills, learn scientific principles of environmental management, and devise solution strategies for ecological problems. Graduates are prepared for careers in ecological research, biodiversity science, nature conservation agencies, sustainable resource management, geographical analysis, landscape planning, and environmental education.

required_degree_domains: Related subjects with fundamental knowledge in Chemistry and Biology

english_requirements.notes: Applicants with a Bachelor's degree from Australia, the United Kingdom, Ireland, Canada, Malta, New Zealand, Singapore, South Africa or the United States do not have to prove their English language skills.

course_content_summary: An interdisciplinary, research-oriented program combining chemistry and biology. The curriculum provides a strong scientific education in modern biomolecular research. Core subjects include organic chemistry, physical chemistry, cell biology, genetics, and biochemistry. Specialization areas in biology include biochemistry, bioinformatics, immunology, biochemical pharmacology, molecular genetics, microbiology, and cell biology. Chemistry focus areas cover bioorganic chemistry, cellular chemistry, proteomics, as well as carbohydrate, protein, and nucleic acids chemistry. The program includes both obligatory theory and laboratory courses.

This research-oriented program focuses on product and process design within the life science industry. Students acquire research qualifications and learn to develop or improve products and processes. Key topics include environmentally-friendly packaging materials, research on food and pharmaceutical products, understanding high-tech research programs, formulating targeted research proposals, and publishing research results.

english_requirements.notes: Minimum B2 level required. For the English Linguistics area of specialisation, C1 is required.

course_content_summary: The Master's in Linguistics is a two-year, 120 ECTS program focusing on general theories and research directions in linguistics. It covers universal structures of human speech, application of linguistic knowledge to individual languages, language universals, and comparative language observations. The program offers six specializations, including General Linguistics, English Linguistics, and a double-degree option in applied linguistics with the Università di Verona. Three specializations can be studied entirely in English.

specific_ects_requirements.condition_comment: Required for direct entry into the 3-semester track. Can be acquired in a pre-semester for applicants with a 180 ECTS Bachelor's degree.

english_requirements.notes: Certification via German Abitur is also accepted.

german_requirements.notes: German skills are not required for the course, but they are highly recommended.

course_content_summary: This program elevates Bachelor's level knowledge to a management perspective, grounded in the St. Gallen Management Approach. Students can choose specializations in International Sales Management, People and Culture, or IT Management. A four-semester option focuses on Digital Leadership and offers a double degree opportunity with Sapienza Università di Roma. The curriculum balances theory and practice through case studies, insights from experienced professors, and a mandatory internship or research project. Key topics include management theories, practical application in business, and preparation for a Master's thesis.

english_requirements.notes: German "Abitur" certificate with proof of six (G8) or seven years (G9) of English language instruction accepted. Higher education entrance qualification obtained in Great Britain, Ireland, USA, Canada, Australia, New Zealand also accepted.

course_content_summary: The Master's programme in Machine Learning offers a flexible curriculum with a wide choice of courses in machine learning and general computer science. Students participate in lectures, seminars, and project lab courses, focusing on both basic and applied research. The program leverages Tübingen's strong research ecosystem, including the Max Planck Institutes for Intelligent Systems and Biological Cybernetics, the Excellence Cluster 'Machine Learning: New Perspectives for Science', the Tübingen AI Center, and Cyber Valley. Key topics cover all major methodological branches of machine learning. The curriculum emphasizes practical application through project work and a Master's thesis, allowing students to develop code for scientific projects. Interdisciplinarity is encouraged, with thesis supervision available from any computer science subfield.

english_requirements.notes: German "Abitur" with at least six (G8) or seven (G9) years of English, a Bachelor's degree in English/American Studies, or a university degree from the UK, Ireland, USA, Canada, Australia, or New Zealand are also accepted.

german_requirements.notes: German is not required.

course_content_summary: The MSc in Management & Economics at the University of Tübingen combines advanced management tools with game-theoretic concepts to analyze strategic business interactions. The curriculum focuses on the strategic behavior of competing firms, managerial economics, personnel economics, and industrial organization. Students develop analytical skills using game theory and principal-agent theory to design efficient incentive systems, select performance measures, and manage human capital. The program prepares graduates to handle changing market conditions, competitor actions, and internal governance by emphasizing systematic analysis, comprehensive decision-making, and flexible adaptation strategies.

accepted_tests.test_name: Universität Mannheim Service und Marketing GmbH language test

english_requirements.notes: A degree with at least 40% taught in English or a University Entrance Qualification from an English-language school serves as proof. Test results must be no more than two years old.

german_requirements.notes: Required only for the German-English track. Not required for the English track.

course_content_summary: The Mannheim Master in Management (MMM) offers flexible, internationally-focused preparation for management careers. Students can specialize in areas such as Accounting and Taxation, Finance, Banking and Insurance, Information Systems, Management, Marketing and Sales, and Operations Management. The curriculum includes core courses like Business Economics I and II, Decision Analysis, Corporate Social Responsibility, and Applied Econometrics or Empirical Methods. Students can create individual schedules, take elective modules, and benefit from a program taught mostly in English. The program supports international experience through semesters abroad and eight double degree programs with prestigious partners like Bocconi, ESSEC, and USC.

program_name: Mannheim Master in Operations and Supply Chain Management

accepted_tests.test_name: Universität Mannheim Service und Marketing GmbH test

english_requirements.notes: A degree with at least 40% of courses and exams in English, or a University Entrance Qualification from an English-language school, serves as proof. Tests must be no more than two years old.

course_content_summary: This program focuses on holistic supply chain management, covering procurement, production, logistics, and service. Key areas include applying descriptive, predictive, and prescriptive analytical methods to solve complex problems. It also integrates global issues like sustainability and digital transformation, equipping students to design and manage efficient and sustainable corporate networks and processes.

program_name: Mannheim Master's in Finance, Accounting and Taxation

accepted_tests.test_name: Universität Mannheim Service und Marketing GmbH language test

english_requirements.notes: Proof can be provided by a study programme with English as language of instruction (at least 40% of courses) or a University Entrance Qualification from a school with English as the language of instruction.

course_content_summary: The program combines corporate finance, accounting, and taxation, focusing on their interaction and influence on strategic decisions. A key emphasis is placed on data analysis and empirical methods, which are crucial for modern finance and tax challenges. The curriculum trains specialists to understand finance from multiple perspectives and to assist companies in making sustainable, well-founded decisions.

specific_ects_requirements.condition_comment: At least one course required with a grade of 2.5 or better.

english_requirements.notes: Proof can also be provided by an English language university entrance exam (e.g., A-levels) or a degree with at least 90 ECTS taught in English.

course_content_summary: The program focuses on applied research in Political Science with an empirical-analytical approach. Key areas include quantitative methodology and the political system of the EU. Specializations cover International Relations, International Political Economy, International Comparative Social Research, Comparative Political Behaviour, Political Sociology, and Quantitative Methods. Course topics include political institutions of European democracies, EU legislation, electoral systems, political communication, participation, coalition theories, European integration, European governance, African politics, conflict studies, and political economy.

program_name: Master of Arts in International Relations & Global Politics | IRGP

german_requirements.notes: German language proficiency is desirable, but it is not a mandatory admission requirement.

course_content_summary: The programme offers an in-depth exploration of global political challenges, focusing on analysing complex issues in international relations and developing research-based solutions. Key topics include security policy, environmental and climate policy, and the role of international organisations. With an interdisciplinary approach, students gain comprehensive knowledge in political science, benefit from hands-on projects, and participate in current research. The curriculum prepares graduates for careers in academia, the public sector, NGOs, or the private sector.

english_requirements.notes: Required for applicants whose native language is not English.

german_requirements.notes: Basic German language is advised.

course_content_summary: This program focuses on the intersection of traditional jewellery craftsmanship and modern technology, research, and development. It emphasizes experimentation, analysis, and critical reflection to foster aesthetic confidence and independent thinking. The curriculum explores the broad concept of jewellery within the contexts of material, technology, body, space, series production versus one-offs, and tradition versus innovation. The interdisciplinary approach connects jewellery to social development, culture, and the environment, targeting motivated jewellery and object makers from related design fields.

program_name: Master of Arts in Marketing and Artificial Intelligence

specific_ects_requirements.condition_comment: At least 50% of the ECTS from the Bachelor’s degree must be in the field of Economics.

english_requirements.notes: for applicants who are not native English speakers

course_content_summary: The program focuses on educating future marketing leaders by integrating traditional marketing with modern technology. Key topics include generative AI, marketing automation, strategic and operational marketing, business case studies, International Marketing, Strategic Marketing, Consumer Behaviour, and Brand Management. Technical skills are developed through Marketing and Social Media Analytics using R and Python, and exploring Conversational AI Applications. The curriculum also covers Creativity, Entrepreneurship, Sustainability, and Cross-Functional Skills. A double degree option with ESC Clermont-Ferrand, France is available.

specific_ects_requirements.domain_name: Quantitative Methods, Empirical Social Research, Statistics or Mathematics
specific_ects_requirements.condition_comment: Quantitative theses are counted with a maximum of 6 ECTS credits towards the required 24 ECTS credits.

english_requirements.notes: Proof can be a degree taught in English, a University Entrance Qualification from an English-speaking school system, or continuous English classes in German secondary school with an average of 11 points (not older than five years).

course_content_summary: This is a research-oriented program focusing on empirical-analytical approaches, quantitative methods, and comparative research. The curriculum emphasizes training in empirical analysis grounded in theory and rigorous methods. Key concentration areas include Family, Education & Work, Migration & Integration, Economy & Social Welfare State, and Social Inequality. Core modules cover Sociological Research Fields, Foundations of Sociological Theory, Cross-Sectional Data Analysis, Longitudinal Data Analysis, and Research Design, culminating in a research project and Master's thesis. All courses are taught in English.

program_name: Master of Arts in Transcultural Studies

english_requirements.notes: Good knowledge of two more languages, including the respective mother tongue, is mandatory.

course_content_summary: A research-oriented, interdisciplinary program focusing on Asia (South and East) and Europe from a transcultural perspective. It examines how cultures are constituted through transformations, entanglements, and circulatory practices. The curriculum provides methodological tools for the integrated study of various records like objects, texts, and digital media. Students choose one of three study foci: Society, Economy, and Governance (SEG); Knowledge, Belief, and Religion (KBR); or Visual, Media, and Material Culture (VMC).

program_name: Master of Arts in Transportation Design

english_requirements.notes: For applicants whose native language is NOT English.

german_requirements.notes: Basic German language is advised.

course_content_summary: The Master of Arts in Transportation Design is tailored for graduates in industrial or transportation design. Taught in English, the program prepares students for leadership roles in the vehicle and automotive industry. Core competencies developed include structuring project teams, planning design projects, and supervising their execution, moving beyond fundamental design work to encompass project management and leadership responsibilities.

english_requirements.notes: Not required if the language of instruction during Bachelor's studies was completely in English (confirmation from university needed). English courses during university leading to a B2 level are also accepted.

german_requirements.notes: No proof of German is required for enrolment. Students must reach level B1 during their studies. The university recommends starting at A2.

course_content_summary: The Biomedical Engineering programme focuses on mathematical, numerical, and software-based solutions for medical engineering problems. Key topics include modelling, simulation, computer mathematics, and systems theory. Students gain practical skills in sensorics, microcontroller programming, signal and image processing, and physiological modelling. The curriculum also covers applications and risks of medical devices, with lectures on artificial organs, membranes, and minimally invasive surgery. A significant component is a research project in the second semester, which can be conducted at the university, a cooperating company, or a hospital, preparing students for their master's thesis.

english_requirements.notes: This is not necessary if your first degree is from a programme that was entirely taught in English.

course_content_summary: The MBA in International Management provides a classical management foundation integrated with future-oriented topics such as Innovation Management, Digital Business, and Sustainable Globalisation. Students can specialize through electives in Marketing, Finance & Accounting, Big Data & Artificial Intelligence, or Management & Business Transformation. The curriculum emphasizes practical application via real-life company projects. It also offers international experiences, including an optional semester abroad at partner universities and a Double Degree option. The program addresses challenges of globalisation, new technologies, and corporate social responsibility in a volatile business environment.

specific_ects_requirements.condition_comment: Required for applicants with a degree in a related discipline (e.g. business, economics, social and political sciences).

english_requirements.notes: An equivalent English language test is accepted. Also waived if applicant has a higher education entrance qualification from an English-speaking country (min. 1 year of school) or has attended a study programme in English for at least one year.

course_content_summary: The Master of Comparative Business Law (MCBL) is a one-year postgraduate program focusing on European and international business law. The curriculum provides an in-depth understanding of business law in the European single market and the global market. Key topics include traditional comparative law studies (legal methodology, common/civil law), and various aspects of business law such as competition law, contract law, and company law. The program also covers the European Union's legal framework and international trade law. The first semester concentrates on European business law, while the second semester emphasizes international business law.

program_name: Master of Engineering in Electrical Engineering

english_requirements.notes: for applicants who are not native English speakers

course_content_summary: The program offers in-depth knowledge in key areas of electrical engineering, focusing on technologies for the digital future, the interface between electrical engineering and computer science for system and process optimization, and the integration of artificial intelligence to develop next-generation intelligent systems. Graduates are prepared for careers in research, development, industry, and public institutions, with potential roles in technical support, production, and service, as well as opportunities for doctoral studies.

english_requirements.notes: Proof can be a previous university degree taught and assessed in English entirely or with at least 12 ECTS in English, including the final thesis.

german_requirements.notes: Not necessary at the time of application. However, proof of basic German proficiency (A1 level) is required by the end of the second semester.

course_content_summary: This program focuses on the challenges of digital transformation, particularly for small and medium-sized companies. Key topics include digital skills, implementation of digital technologies, shortening innovation and R&D cycles, and effective change management strategies. It develops essential management and leadership skills, emphasizing interdisciplinary thinking to liaise between diverse stakeholders. A core component is business analytics, providing competencies in analyzing, implementing, and using data generated from the digitization of processes.

program_name: Master of Peace Research and International Relations

specific_ects_requirements.condition_comment: Recommended

course_content_summary: This program enables students to analyze complex problems of conflict, war, peace, and international relations. The curriculum focuses on essential theories, methods, and empirical issues in the field. Core modules include Theoretical and Methodological Perspectives, Normative and Critical Perspectives, Global Governance, and Analysing Armed Conflicts. The course emphasizes research-based learning, applying theoretical knowledge to current case studies, and developing epistemological judgement and methodological skills. Students can specialize through elective courses in areas like international law and peace education. Practical experience is offered through excursions such as National Model United Nations, Peace Boat, and workshops on mediation and negotiation.

program_name: Master of Science in Advanced Materials and Manufacturing (Research Master's)
course_content_summary: This research-focused Master's program centers on Advanced Materials and Manufacturing, with a special emphasis on Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning. The curriculum involves students developing their own research topic, supported by two specific elective lectures and two methodological courses during the first two semesters. The program is designed for students with backgrounds in natural sciences and technology and culminates with the completion of a Master's thesis in the third semester.

program_name: Master of Science in Applied Photonics

english_requirements.notes: for non-English native speakers

course_content_summary: This program focuses on Photonics, also known as optical technology, a key 21st-century innovation driver. Core topics include the generation, use, shaping, and transmission of light.
